programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. Heatons first suspect provides an alibi for one of the cold case murders and finds himself in the clear as forensic evidence establishes that all three girls were killed in the same manner. Desperate for his freedom, he cooperates with the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims. When the real killer is named, the race is on to find him before he strikes again. James Gaddas guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a cab driver is badly beaten and his takings stolen, an electronic travel card found near the scene provides a lead, linking the crime to a volunteer worker at a support centre on the Jasmine Allen estate. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A teenager is found unconscious in the street following a party that went disastrously wrong when a known drug dealer turned up causing trouble. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate investigates a knife attack on young girl Aisha, only for the case to take on a personal note when he discovers his new love interest, nurse Maria Joseph, agreed not to report the injury when she treated the wounded girl in hospital. programme.desc: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover. But does that mean record profit or history making loss?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads off to Blackpool to pick up a couple of unusual vehicles - a red 1970 Opel GT and a 1956 Ford Hot Rod pickup

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek meets the owner and builder of a sporty vintage Jaguar special, a Jaguar Viking

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Bradshaw travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course at the Lee Valley White Water Centre, built for the London Olympics in 2012. Travelling north, the presenter reaches Hatfield and the country estate of the queen of romance and author of 723 books, Barbara Cartland. In Stevenage, Michael learns how, in 1935, a new enterprise boosted production of the nations daily loaf with a factory in the town.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism. In Cambridge, he revisits his former university to hear about a treacherous time in its past

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his 1930s Bradshaws inspired tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Crewe, a town steeped in railway history, to investigate the making of cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s. He then heads to Chester to discover the interwar origins of Britains most popular zoo, then goes across the border to the Welsh village of Gresford, scene of one of Britains worst mining disasters

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks back in his old stomping ground of Luton, to meet an old schoolmate who is saying goodbye to his 1912 De Dion Bouton

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks off to West Yorkshire to extract a 1987 Peugeot 205 GTI from a garage in a narrow lane. Its one of the more prized 1.9s and has been stored safely away for many years

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: How the Japanese air force dropped swarms of fleas on a Chinese city in a bid to infect the population with bubonic plague

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Sefton Park Palm House in Liverpool, and discovers the history of the house. Finds include a rare double Ming Dynasty vase bought in a charity shop, some valuable Beatles memorabilia, a Grand Tour Bronze, some unusual Finnish glass, a painting by George Melly, and a silver disc brought in by one of the members of Gerry and the Pacemakers.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: At Brodie Castle in Morayshire, Fiona Bruce meets a Highland Games commentator who has brought along a collection of items including a weighty shot put, a heavy hammer and a brooch presented by Queen Victoria in 1848. Ceramics expert Serhat Ahmet uncovers a brush pot found at the bottom of the sea, while Matthew Haley takes a closer look at a book written by Bram Stoker discovered in a charity shop

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team take Dereks advice when he sniffs out the rarest car theyve ever taken on - the last remaining Hillman GT

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team lift the shutters on their biggest restoration to date in the shape of a Series 1 Land Rover. But does that mean record profit or history making loss?

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: How the Japanese air force dropped swarms of fleas on a Chinese city in a bid to infect the population with bubonic plague

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A journey around the region, examining the challenges currently facing the nations in the area. Simon begins in Malta, uncovering the countrys reputation for organised crime, before moving on to the southern coast of Italy, where he joins police officers exploring underground tunnels used by crime bosses on the run. Finally, he arrives in Albania and discovers an ancient tradition of revenge where children can pay the price for their parents actions

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: The end of the war sees Rene, Edith and the rest of the gang welcoming in the Allied troops, and they soon find that the invasion party includes a couple of old friends

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks off to West Yorkshire to extract a 1987 Peugeot 205 GTI from a garage in a narrow lane. Its one of the more prized 1.9s and has been stored safely away for many years

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos 1930s Bradshaws-inspired railway tour continues in north Wales. In Colwyn Bay, Michael hears how an army of civil servants from the Ministries of Food and Agriculture in London invaded the quiet seaside town, before visiting an amusement park in the holiday resort of Rhyl. Crossing the Menai Strait by the Britannia Bridge, Michael arrives on Anglesey and the elegant seat of the Marquesses of the island

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his 1936 Bradshaws-inspired railway tour of north Wales. He begins in the coastal city of Bangor, where he hears how, when war broke out in September 1939, 2,000 children from the cities of northern England were evacuated by train to north Wales. Heading inland alongside the River Conwy, Michael reaches Tal y Cafn, before investigating the worst dam disaster in Welsh history in Dolgarrog. Finally in Betws y Coed, he attempts to climb one of Snowdonias lesser peaks

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the Welsh mountains of Snowdonia, Michael Portillo reaches an abandoned mine with an art-related history. He also explores the harbour town of Porthmadog, and the fantasy village Portmeirion, crafted by architect Bertram Clough Williams-Ellis, and made famous by The Prisoner TV series

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the elegant coastal town of Aberystwyth, Michael Portillo visits the National Library of Wales, set high on a hill with views over Cardigan Bay. His tour of North Wales draws to a close in style at the birthplace of the nations first classical music festival, Gregynog Hall, near Newtown

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits a vast airplane salvage yard. With some unusual buys in the van, he has his work cut out to re-invent them into design pieces he hopes will fly off the shelf

programme.title: Restoration Workshop

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: The workshop gets a new wall and floor while Sam Lovegrove mends Doms vintage motorbike for a cliff top trial

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: The adventurer sets off on a journey through the continent, beginning in the remote and little-visited north-east. In Venezuela, Simon meets some of the thousands of migrants fleeing the economic collapse of their country, while he learns that neighbouring Guyana could be on the verge of its own oil boom. He also meets a gun-toting former warlord who is now one of Surinames most successful businessmen, and ends his journey in French Guiana, where he goes to the launch site of the European Space Agency

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: Simon starts his travels through Brazil in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests. In Manaus, the city at the heart of the Amazon, he visits a neglected indigenous neighbourhood and meets a nurse who was the only source of healthcare during the pandemic. Simon ends this leg of his journey on the west coast in the city of Rio de Janeiro, where the government has built a hi-tech control room to monitor all parts of the city for potential disasters such as floods

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A beautiful looking sports car has been fooling the punters and intriguing Derek. Its a Challenger E-Type - a fibreglass replica created from an E-Type mould

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Theres Hollywood glamour in the air as Derek heads out to pick up a Rolls-Royce that once belonged to Fred Astaire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Bradshaw travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course at the Lee Valley White Water Centre, built for the London Olympics in 2012. Travelling north, the presenter reaches Hatfield and the country estate of the queen of romance and author of 723 books, Barbara Cartland. In Stevenage, Michael learns how, in 1935, a new enterprise boosted production of the nations daily loaf with a factory in the town.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism. In Cambridge, he revisits his former university to hear about a treacherous time in its past

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his 1930s Bradshaws inspired tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham

programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A patient is admitted with a fever and a swollen arm covered in black lesions, but Mike is nowhere to be seen. Blood tests confirm a case of anthrax, prompting an investigation by the Public Health Officer. Elsewhere, a specialist is summoned when a womans dizzy spells cause concern, while Alun and Lizzie are intrigued by the mysterious appearance of a young woman

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The former Republic of Ireland midfielder talks about his life in professional football and his battles with addiction

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2002, the year of Roy Keanes famous spat with Mick McCarthy, igniting a national debate that continues to this day. Bertie Ahern led Fianna Fail to victory in the general election but fell just short of an overall majority and a levy of 15 per cent was introduced on plastic bags

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Headline stories from 2003, accompanied by the music of the time

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as the remaining four famous faces compete in the semi-final as they take on healthy dishes. The two stars who fail to impress the judges will end up in the cook-off, with the pair fighting it out for the last place in the grand final

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Chinook, a fluffy Samoyed dog, is brought in to see if the tumour on his liver is operable. Mirabella the cat arrives for a radical radium treatment that only the vets can provide in a last-ditch effort to treat her thyroid issues. Ruas owner is concerned he may have epilepsy, while a bull comes to the hospital for a delicate operation

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Filthy House Swap
programme.desc: Two families swap homes in this one-off social experiment, as they discover whether taking on another persons messy house can help them get to grips with their own grime

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the second part, he discusses becoming only the second player to reach 200 appearances for Munster and how he dealt with anxiety and mental health throughout his rugby career

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Louie Spence plays mystery host as he throws a dinner party for four strangers at his home. All they have to do is guess who has been cooking for them to win a cash prize and leave the dancer doing his own washing up. But if he manages to keep his identity intact, theyll be the ones pulling on the Marigolds. Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In a remote part of the Amazon, a corpse has been recovered bearing extensive and unusual bruising and Jeremy Wade hears further reports of a terrifying predator that cannot be caught with a rod and line. Determined to investigate further, the extreme angler straps on an air tank and explores the murky waters

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Amanda from Colchester, Alan from Cardiff, Paawan from Leicester and Tom from Solihull pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team continue their mission to prove to the nation that it is possible achieve a dream home renovation at little cost. Each family involved has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: Luis hosts a pizza party in the hopes of building bridges for the Gorga and Giudice families. Later, Jackie starts to question Dolores loyalty

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: A look at the start of the knockout phase of the 2019/20 UEFA Champions League season, with the referees having to deal with the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry presents a documentary looking at the impact of the seasons on wildlife, with first edition examining winter in the northern hemisphere and summer in the southern. On Norwegian archipelago Svalbards frozen sea-ice, a polar bear mother prepares her cub for a life alone, while Marion Island in the Indian Ocean between South Africa and Antarctica becomes home to a million penguins as breeding season starts

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets enthusiastic collie greyhound cross Bud, who holds the record as Batterseas most unwanted resident, having been at the kennels for more than two years. He has boundless energy and needs an owner to match, so Paul enters him into a doggie fun run, hoping he will catch the eye of the athletic pet-lovers. He also helps spaniel Willow, who was found tied to a tree outside Batterseas Old Windsor site in a dreadful condition, and acts as masseur to Jenson the staffie, who suffered accidental scarring that has left his back dry and itchy

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul is in puppy heaven when he meets a litter of newborn Staffie cross pups - but things turn serious when three of them fall sick and are rushed into intensive care. The presenter also gets to grips with feisty collie pup Tilly, assisted by a 13-year-old girl visiting Battersea with the Make-a-Wish foundation, and helps doddery 16-year-old Tiny, a Yorkie whose arthritis has the medical team worried

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Carolines father Henry suggests turning off her life-support machine, presenting Dr Ormerod with a dilemma. Goodwin puts his career on the line by allowing a holistic therapist to treat a back injury and student nurse Samantha Beaumont is less than impressed by a date with theatre technician Alun Morris. Medical drama, starring Robert Daws, Michael Jayston and Amy Robbins

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Goodwins medical skills are brought into question when he wrongly accuses a cafe of making a local jazz band ill and a dinner party turns murderous for Ormerod and Weatherill when their host reveals his wife is trying to kill him. The Health Authority announce plans to reorganise management at the Royal, leaving Mr Harper less than keen to reapply for his job, so Ken and Alun hit upon a plan to convince him otherwise

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2006

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories looking back at the headlines of the past, focusing here on 2007

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enchantress Cassie Nightingale returns with her now-teenage daughter Grace, who has inherited her powers of intuition. Cassie and Sams wedding is just days away and the town of Middleton prepares for this highly anticipated event. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Part two of two. Cassie and Sams long-awaited wedding has finally arrived. Sam receives some help from Grace in finding the Forever Tree. However, the wedding brings up complex emotions for Grace. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2010

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2011

programme.title: Filthy Homes from Hell
programme.desc: A peek inside dirty and dismal dwellings, including Britains most insanitary student digs, which boast a bath coated in body fat, and a macabre zoo housing all manner of creatures. There is also a listed building that has not been cleaned for 400 years, the tragic story of a woman whose devotion to dogs cost her everything and the UKs worst rat infestation

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: With all the emergency agencies involved in a simulated plane crash exercise, real problems place airport operations in jeopardy

programme.title: Vicky Phelan: In Her Own Words
programme.desc: An interview with the medical campaigner, who talks about the 221+ support group, her fight for life-saving treatment, and finding happiness in the toughest of times

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: LA
programme.sub-title: To Sell or Not to Sell
programme.desc: Tracys latest listing yields an interesting new development, and Josh Altman and Heather clash after having different reactions to an offer.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a £50,000 prize

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New Jersey
programme.desc: When Jackie goes to treatment for her eating disorder and receives life-threatening news, Dolores plots how to get back at her for questioning her loyalty

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Tom continues to spiral over his mistakes while juggling devastating news from back home. Captain Glenn and the rest of the deck team contemplate whether they can trust him

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: During a night of strong winds, Parsifal runs aground and gets stuck heeled over, sending the boat into chaos. As the incident was on Toms anchor watch, Captain Glenn has a word

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Lala and Katie freak out at Raquel for making out with Oliver. Charlis arrival in Lake Havasu helps Raquel find her voice but leads to a friendship-ending fight

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to revisit some of the most memorable moments from Gogglebox Ireland, demonstrating why Irelands resident telly addicts have developed a reputation for eccentricity. Includes highlights featuring the Tully twins, Angela and Eileen, Anita and Tracey, the Adenugas and the Ryans

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The detective and his team are called to the site of a fatal crash involving an articulated lorry, a car and a blue pick-up truck. An unexpected discovery found on the victim at the scene soon suggests there could be far more to this particular accident than meets the eye, sparking an unsettling chain of events that leads Grace and Branson on a game of cat and mouse through Brighton to save an innocent life before time runs out

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Secrets of the Universe
programme.desc: The ultimate guide to the universe, as told by the passionate scientists, engineers and astronauts behind the biggest missions in space exploration

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Police mount a special operation across the airport, and passenger experience manager Taz rushes to the aid of a passenger on her way to Warsaw who takes a tumble on an escalator. Elsewhere, a five-star airline tries to make a comeback with its business class

programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Police detain a passenger whose bag is suspected to contain three firearms, Virgin has a launch party for their new route to the live music capital of the world - Austin, Texas, and passenger experience manager Hannah rushes to the aid of a seasoned traveller who takes a funny turn

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell meet Sara Hathaway who, after losing her adoptive mother and older brother in the space of just a year, is desperate to find her birth family. Plus, Diane Kerridge shares her story of being forced to give up the child she gave birth to as a teenager in the 1960s. Over 50 years later, she is hoping to find her long lost son

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Matt from North Somerset, Sangita from Manchester, Ryan from Essex and Johanna from Renfrewshire pit their wits against one of the Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Alvin originally from Blackburn, Liz from Farnham, Bairbre from Wetherby and Roy from Manchester pit their wits against one of the Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds

programme.title: Time Is Money
programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. Each player stands before an hourglass-shaped money timer that begins to drain as the questions are asked - and the only way of stopping it is to answer correctly. Once the money is gone, the contestant is out - and only one can make the final to play for the amount they have managed to save

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team continue their mission to prove to the nation that it is possible achieve a dream renovation at little cost. Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as two households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week and each has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Ben Bailey Smith, Claire Richards and Lisa Riley all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross tries to prevent Rachel discovering he slept with Chloe from the photocopying shop, but she finds out anyway and a major argument ensues at her apartment. Trapped in Monicas bedroom, the other friends cannot help overhearing everything

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Without the Ski Trip
programme.desc: Ross and Rachels separation begins to cause friction among the pals, who feel they have to choose between the estranged pair - and an impending skiing holiday only makes matters worse. Jennifer Aniston and David Schwimmer star

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy goes into a downward spiral, while Marlon has an idea and also makes a discovery

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggies slip of the tongue arouses Bens suspicions, George nervously awaits the verdict in court, Jodie jumps to Bethanys defence, and Debbies loved ones are optimistic ahead of her appeal

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: With hours until the suspected attack on a university, John finds himself alone and in captivity. If he is to save the lives of those at risk, he must find a means of escape without alerting the men who are holding him and the allies whom he can no longer trust

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Tom Allen and his mum Irene, Fleur East and her mum Irene, and Steve Pemberton and his daughter Maddie compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events of the desperate 14-day search by Cambridgeshire Police to the eventual discovery of their bodies

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.sub-title: The Sea Croft, Lytham St Annes
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Sea Croft, a struggling BandB in Lytham St Annes, Lancashire. Two years ago, owner Georgina gave up a career teaching business studies with a dream of running a successful guest house. Now, a £50,000 bill for unforeseen repairs and dismal occupancy has left her struggling to run the business. With her immaculate rooms lying empty, self-confessed neat freak Georgina cannot understand where she is going wrong. Alex can see the potential for success but getting Georgina to stop focusing on little details and start seeing the bigger picture does not prove easy

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of pioneering modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazing engineers who designed new building techniques, took risks on untested materials and revolutionised their field. The first programme focuses on the aircraft carrier

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall hosts the show in which participants tackle a personal challenge, and seek to complete it within 12 months. They include new parents who want to transform the life of their deaf baby girl, and a military couple desperate to start a family. Cameras also follow a mother and daughter who hope to lose 15 stone between them, and an oncology student who wants to become Miss England

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Sharon on reception has pulled the short straw as three flightloads of Brits are expected at the same time, and to make matters worse there is a problem with the booking system. Five friends from Norfolk have arrived for some much-needed fun in the sun and hotel entertainer Tom is poolside to offer them a game of Human Target

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: More than 70,000 racegoers pack Aintree in blazing sunshine for the Grand National, the peoples race with one million pounds in prize money at stake. While Irish trainer Willie Mullins bids for back-to-back wins, new trainer Michael Keady, who has never had a winner over jumps, dreams of a fairy-tale ending with 100/1 outsider Horantzau DAiry. Is a new Grand National legend about to be born?

programme.title: Live Racing
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents live coverage of a meeting headlined by the Queens Cup from Musselburgh, plus the finals of the Challenger Series from Haydock Park. With analysis from Leonna Mayor and Adele Mulrennan, reporting by Matt Chapman and Natalie Green, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million. However, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week.

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: Leah has spent almost two months nursing a patient from the same farming community as her recover after surgery. Its a sad but proud moment when hes finally well enough to leave. Despite trying to stay out of trouble, nurse Mikey faces all the ward banter as suspect number one when a vital medicine machine malfunctions. Meanwhile, Caitlin faces her first shift in Resus as a qualified nurse. She holds her own well, but has a patient whose case illustrates just how fast things can take a turn in AandE

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of todays performers be just what the pair are looking for?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Toby Anstis, Paula Radcliffe, Rav Wilding and Vanessa Feltz pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor James McAvoy, who talks about his directorial debut, The Morning Show star Gugu Mbatha-Raw, discussing her crime thriller Fuze, actress, singer and producer Rita Wilson, chatting about her sixth studio album, and comedian Russell Howard, whos promoting his stand-up tour Dont Tell the Algorithm

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits Horley near Gatwick to help Quasim, the owner, improve his ailing Surrey guesthouse. With 13 other BandBs all battling for the same trade, he is struggling to make the monthly repayments. Alex can see the potential in Quasims business, but curbing his expensive tastes to maximise profits turns out to be far from easy

programme.title: Vigilante and Proud: Catching the Street Thieves
programme.desc: Street crime is rising across Britain, often going unsolved. Ex-detective Rebecca Mason helps victims track down offenders using tech, community insight and detective work

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny The Vixen Ryan, Anne The Governess Hegerty, Shaun The Dark Destroyer Wallace, Mark The Beast Labbett and Paul The Sinnerman Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by actor James McAvoy, who talks about his directorial debut, The Morning Show star Gugu Mbatha-Raw, discussing her crime thriller Fuze, actress, singer and producer Rita Wilson, chatting about her sixth studio album, and comedian Russell Howard, whos promoting his stand-up tour Dont Tell the Algorithm

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Tom Allen and his mum Irene, Fleur East and her mum Irene, and Steve Pemberton and his daughter Maddie compete in the comedy game show. The parents choose which questions their children will answer and gamble on how many they can get right. If the contestants meet expectations they will get the money, but fall short and the cash is split between the other teams

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny The Vixen Ryan, Anne The Governess Hegerty, Shaun The Dark Destroyer Wallace, Mark The Beast Labbett and Paul The Sinnerman Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Dublin After Dark
programme.desc: Efforts of members of An Garda Síochána to combat growing levels of anti-social behaviours on the streets of the capital. Garda Shauna Naughton and Garda Jun Han monitor the streets of Dublin on their nightly patrols in some of the worst hot spots for crime

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: The pressure is on when strange things start happening to Cassie, Abigail and Joys abilities. Fantasy, starring Catherine Bell

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Qinghai-Tibet line, the highest railway in the world, providing an insight into the engineering challenges faced at high altitude and in bitterly cold temperatures

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following life at the Spanish Rio Park hotel and the holiday-makers to whom they cater. The first edition sees the staff ready to welcome thousands of Brits, but no sooner have they started than there is a problem when the lifts break down and they race to get them repaired

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler and underwater detective Jeremy Wade goes in search of the real creature behind the enduring legend of the sea serpent - starting with a violent attack in the English Channel, and culminating in the dark depths of the Mediterranean Sea

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: As Frances and Joyce battle for leadership of the new group, the others face problems of a much more personal nature. Erica and Wills relationship is tested after his cancer diagnosis, Pat is struggling with her unhappy marriage and Miriam has one last chance to stop David enlisting. Newcomer Teresa Fenchurch gets off to a bad start, but her mishap brings a dashing RAF officer to the village. Francesca Annis and Samantha Bond star, with Frances Grey, Ed Stoppard, Claire Rushbrook and Leanne Best

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick faces a dilemma after one of his colleagues is suspected of robbery and Greengrass develops a keen interest in a villagers greyhound. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Diet crashers Emma and Kaye have 10 days to slim down for a day at the races, and Emma tries out the baby food diet, consuming up to 14 jars of it a day. Eve and Kate go on six-week dieting plans before their holidays, with Eve trying out a bland diet designed to minimise gut irritation with low-fibre and low-fat foods, while Kate is given the champagne diet and can indulge in one glass of bubbly per day, accompanied by strictly calorie-controlled meals

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Sue Tipton bought the Gungate Hotel in Tamworth, Staffordshire, on impulse because she was looking for a challenge, but the last few years have become a constant struggle and she has been forced to run the business virtually single-handed. Alex Polizzi spends a night to get a feel for what guests encounter and the next day delivers a hard-hitting verdict. Can Sue handle the truth?

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy reaches his breaking point, while Jacob is determined and Marlon helps one of his friends

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggie faces a grilling for assaulting Megan, Todd thinks on his feet when Theo discovers his escape bag, Ken grows concerned at Daniels bitterness, and Bethany queries Jodies credentials

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: After finding out about the hospitals staffing problems, Jill offers to cut short her maternity leave and return to work, while Matron notices that Catherine is doing her best to avoid Adam. Out in Africa, Jeff faces the horrors of war and makes a promise to a dying woman - but faces difficulties in keeping his word

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frosts investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An attempted murder leads Nick to uncover a local landowners shady past, while reports of illegal boxing bouts keep his fellow coppers on their toes. Meanwhile, Kate discusses the reason for Alex Ferrenbys increasingly erratic behaviour

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Amy is trapped and running out of time. On land, Kirsten chases down the traitors accomplice. When the traitor on Vigil puts their plans into action, the crew are forced to fight for their lives. Conclusion of the crime drama, starring Suranne Jones

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggies slip of the tongue arouses Bens suspicions, George nervously awaits the verdict in court, Jodie jumps to Bethanys defence, and Debbies loved ones are optimistic ahead of her appeal

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy goes into a downward spiral, while Marlon has an idea and also makes a discovery

programme.title: Prime Suspect
programme.desc: DSI Jane Tennison approaches retirement but vows to solve one final case before stepping down - the disappearance of a teenager. Unfortunately, the task is complicated by her alcoholism and concerns about her ailing father. Two-part drama, starring Helen Mirren, Stephen Tompkinson, Brendan Coyle, Frank Finlay and Tom Bell

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Robert Rinder examines the murders of police officers Nicola Hughes and Fiona Bone in 2012, hearing about their fathers efforts to fundraise in their memories

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield forces panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jewellers shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. Vernon tempts fate by employing a new mechanic for the garage without asking Bernie and attempts to recreate a traditional dance at the town fete. Guest starring Hannah Gordon

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain is determined, while Matty delivers devastating news and Tracy is feeling heartbroken

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim may have been poisoned, while Paddy makes a mess of things and Grahams suspicions are aroused

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain feels he has been betrayed, while Joe is annoyed and Marlon is put out

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy reaches his breaking point, while Jacob is determined and Marlon helps one of his friends

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy goes into a downward spiral, while Marlon has an idea and also makes a discovery

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets enthusiastic collie greyhound cross Bud, who holds the record as Batterseas most unwanted resident, having been at the kennels for more than two years. He has boundless energy and needs an owner to match, so Paul enters him into a doggie fun run, hoping he will catch the eye of the athletic pet-lovers. He also helps spaniel Willow, who was found tied to a tree outside Batterseas Old Windsor site in a dreadful condition, and acts as masseur to Jenson the staffie, who suffered accidental scarring that has left his back dry and itchy

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates the disappearance of a teacher from a secretive public school, and begins to question who he can trust when a body is discovered. Thursday wrestles with the imminent closure of Cowley Station as he tries to find the vital evidence that will connect Eddie Nero to a series of unsolved murders, and assigns Fancy to track down the man who may be the key to cracking the case

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The stories of two sons who were given up for adoption and the families who have stopped at nothing to find them. Andrew Barlow was adopted as a baby, but his birth mother Patricia Clark refused to sign the adoption papers and never gave up looking for him, while as a child, Julie Redman discovered that her mother Joan had given up her older brother for adoption

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the team at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza prepare to throw a party to show off the hotel to local travel agents, the Spanish resort is hit by a rare rainstorm, resulting in the hotel springing leaks and causing the electricity to cut out. Meanwhile, a 14-strong friendship group party in memory of their dear friend, and a couple celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary at Benidorms famously romantic waterfalls

programme.title: The Secret Life of Our Pets
programme.desc: How pets demonstrate their incredible athletic abilities evolved from their wild origins, from dogs that can outrun human athletes and a Californian cat who loves to surf, to the extraordinary high-speed flight abilities of parrots, budgies and cockatiels. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson advises Keith and Val Morris on ways to revamp their failing hotel and conference centre the Hanmer Arms, which is losing money despite being large and well-equipped. She explains why the lack of a full-time receptionist and permanent head chef mean problems with continuity - and urges the reluctant and negative owners to boost their dwindling bookings by attracting corporate clients

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The team investigates a case where nothing is ever as it first appears as an unidentified murder victim is found left out in the open countryside in curious circumstances. The post-mortem reveals more questions than it does answers, and as Grace works to identify the body, a chilling discovery in central Brighton suggests that a brazen killer is orchestrating an elaborate quest for vengeance

programme.title: Beyond the Blackboard (2011)
programme.desc: A newly qualified teacher sets up a makeshift classroom for homeless teenagers. Initially afraid and suspicious of her students, she gradually gets to know them and becomes determined to make a difference to their difficult lives. Fact-based drama, starring Emily VanCamp, Steve Talley and Treat Williams

programme.title: Chicago PD
programme.desc: The unit receives a ransom note demanding the immediate release of imprisoned drugs kingpin Pulpo in exchange for the release of Antonios son Diego. As the detectives work to secure the youngsters safety, it becomes clear that Voight is taking orders from someone outside the Chicago PD and appears to have an agenda of his own. Crime drama, starring Jason Beghe and Jon Seda

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, revealing how ground-breaking forensic technology was used to track down the killer of 87-year-old Gladys Godfrey, who was brutally murdered in her own home. Plus, male domestic abuse victim Ian McNicholl tells the story of his suffering at the hands of his ex-girlfriend

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Jonny fights to win Bex back while unaware of her secret, Dylan faces a huge decision about his future, and Naisha finds the strength to end her relationship.

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: Geeta is dreading the bleep test, but she soon has a dilemma that overshadows that. Bob gets in touch with his inner Spider-Man and Paul gets a final warning. Comedy set in a police training college, starring Sarah Parish and Mark Heap

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Hannah, John, Helen and Vince vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Paul from Glasgow, Denise from Hampshire, Louise from Nottingham and Colin from Surrey pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Sam, Pauline, Rachel and Mark pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Chris, Trevor, Fiona and Susie

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Patrick, Sheldon and Liam compete for the affections of Leeds singleton Elli, hoping to land a date based on their fashion choices

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: With the Benidorm Plaza full to bursting, and housekeeping pushed to their limits, the clock is ticking as bride-to-be Hannah and her hen party are keen to check into their rooms to get the party started. Meanwhile, restaurant manager Pepe is challenged by new hotel manager Angel to revamp his weekly Gala Dinner

programme.title: Storage Hoarders
programme.desc: Aggie MacKenzie is joined by antiques expert Gillian Anderson Price to help two more compulsive collectors, including a music enthusiast whose passion for instruments has spiralled out of control. The presenter also stumbles upon a special James Bond toy car

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Heathrow: Britains Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. This time, staff at the airport deal with the aftermath of a polar vortex. Meanwhile, officers Dave and Terry are on the hunt for a wanted man who has gone missing on a plane, and Ian and Steve battle in a snow blizzard as they attempt to keep the runways open

programme.title: Live League of Ireland
programme.desc: Galway United v Derry City (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the Premier Division match at Eamonn Deacy Park. Galway finished in eighth place last season, only avoiding having to play in the promotion/relegation play-off on goal difference, while Derry were runners-up to Shamrock Rovers, but both teams have made similarly inconsistent starts to the current campaign

programme.title: Bump
programme.desc: Angie begins her gruelling course of treatment at the hospital, supported every step of the way by her family, for better and worse

programme.title: Bump
programme.desc: Dom and Tim finally open their record store, but their friendship is tested on opening night. Santi falls out with Vince again, and Bowie rediscovers his passion for acting

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined by former England cricket captain Freddie Flintoff, The White Lotus star Jason Isaacs, comedian Rebel Wilson, renowned actor David Oyelowo and Bafta-winner Sophie Willan. Plus, music by Wet Leg

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Dannii Minogue hosts an all-female counterpart to dating show I Kissed a Boy, welcoming 10 single women to a masseria in Italy in search of love - introducing themselves to each other with a kiss. As the group celebrates their first night, hearts race for two girls who take no time in exploring the chemistry outside of their match

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: Now that Julie is closing in on the infiltrator, she is the frontrunner for Cunninghams job, but Melanie has a plan to discredit her. Has Leggo gone too far with Daz?

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: The new head of training is set to be announced, Pauls life is about to get even more stressful, while Steph and Mike might possibly be getting back together

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: Despite her manager screwing up a gig and her ex moving on, Jann thinks things are looking up when a new manager promises to re-ignite her career. Canadian comedy series, starring singer-songwriter Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: Jann
programme.desc: The singer learns she is only part of the chorus for a charity single. Canadian comedy, starring Jann Arden as a fictionalised version of herself

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: A review of the latest matches in Europes premier club competition, plus a look ahead to forthcoming fixtures

programme.title: Britains Brightest Family
programme.desc: The Gavins from Gloucestershire and the Curtises from Kent battle for a place in the quarter-finals of the knockout tournament in which 16 families compete to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britains Brightest Family

programme.title: Britains Brightest Family
programme.desc: The Pettigrew family from Scotland and the Osholiki family from Dorset battle for a place in the quarter-finals. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including making quails egg wrapped in a nest of potato. Sheree Murphy hosts

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Actress and former glamour model Linda Lusardi takes on the diners, who are hoping to end their run on a high point after suffering a turbulent week. Will it be a photo-finish for the ex-beauty queen or will her opponents grab the cash? Presented by Brian McFadden

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three single men in Cardiff try to win the affection of Ben Brown - also known as Mr Gay Wales 2017 - by each buying their potential date an outfit they think he might like

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Paul Walker and Victoria Maude leave their five-bedroom house in Arun Valley, West Sussex behind to trade places with Steve and Naz Edney, who live in a three-bedroom house in a Fareham, Hampshire, and are in the countrys poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Cleanfluencer Brandon turns cleaning up into a tidy business, Tati and her crew tackle a filthy house and Just for Kicks revive some battered trainers

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes look at one of the UKs busiest transport hubs, chronicling the issues that face station staff, transport workers, emergency services, commuters and tourists

programme.title: Gary Barlows Wine Tour
programme.desc: The Take That star explores the country, searching for the most authentic, accessible and exciting food and drink experiences South Africa has to offer. He begins his adventure by meeting friend and fellow musician Eliot Kennedy in Franschhoek, heading to the Spier Wine Farm for a crash course in the countrys most celebrated grape varieties before sampling some classic South African food, cooked on a braai

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Ben Bailey Smith, Claire Richards and Lisa Riley all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: Documentary series about pawnbrokers and their customers, and the impact the economic crisis has had on the trade. In the first episode, a businessman tries to sell his prized Lamborghini to veteran pawnbroker Ray, who knows he can only afford to take the car if he can find a buyer. A woman tries to sell a watch that could be valuable to a life-changing amount - but could also be a fake. A former 1980s pop star fallen on hard times visits a pawnbroker to raise some much-needed funds

programme.title: Side Hustlers
programme.desc: The Hustlers present their final pitches to Ashley and Emma, hoping for an investment. Who has what it takes to turn their side hustle into their main business?

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to revisit some of the most memorable moments from Gogglebox Ireland, demonstrating why Irelands resident telly addicts have developed a reputation for eccentricity. Includes highlights featuring the Tully twins, Angela and Eileen, Anita and Tracey, the Adenugas and the Ryans

programme.title: Dark Side of Reality TV
programme.desc: The reality show Kid Nation, in which 40 children were tasked with creating their own society in the New Mexico desert. It quickly devolved into a chaotic and challenging situation, drawing comparisons to Lord of the Flies

programme.title: Iain Stirlings CelebAbility
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffat leads three friends looking to win a host of fabulous prizes by defeating Verona Rose, Naughty Boy, Shaughna Phillips and Stephen Bailey

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Reunited with old friends, the train throws a party. Alliances are tested as the truth is finally revealed

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes another batch of celebrity guests to his green room, including star of stage and screen Brian Cox, Mad Men actress Christina Hendricks, Dancing on Ice judge Oti Mabuse and Strictly champion Chris McCausland. Plus, music by Snow Patrol
